hypothesis: If a 3D molecular encoder is trained with a CLIP/SigLIP-style contrastive objective to align electron-density grids with pose-agnostic SMILES embeddings (without explicit SE(3) constraints), then it will learn pose-invariant representations that outperform unimodal and SE(3)-equivariant baselines on retrieval under unseen rotations and downstream property prediction, because the multimodal alignment supplies rotation-invariant supervision that encourages the 3D encoder to discard pose information.
